Filter customers

Customer pricing

One row per customer, shared live with your whole team. Kegs are grouped together, cartons are grouped together (30L kegs sit next to their standard keg). Pick a deal in any cell — it saves immediately for everyone, rounded up to the nearest dollar. Use the Bulk button on a row to set one deal across all of that customer's kegs or cartons at once. Small grey text shows the last price actually paid, for reference. The Status column tracks whether a customer's pricing has been updated and whether the RSM has met with them. Only SKU groups enabled on the Product List Prices page show up here.

SKU group list prices

One list price per SKU group (ex GST/WET) — this is what every customer deal is calculated from, shared live with your team. Kegs are priced per keg; cartons are priced per carton. Each carton SKU has its own cans/carton, cans/multipack and can size — set these accurately, since the margin calculators use them directly (a wrong carton size will throw off every margin figure for that beer). Deal prices below are rounded up to the nearest whole dollar. Use the "Enabled" toggle to control which SKU groups appear on the Pricing Grid, temporary deals, and customer summaries.
EnabledSKU groupFormatPack sizes (cartons only)List priceDeal price reference (for invoicing)Updated

Choose a customer

Choose a customer

Set this customer's retail glass sizes/prices and single-can prices, then see the margin on each keg beer or carton-as-single at their current (or a "what-if") deal. Changing the deal here does not commit it — use Apply deal to save it as their standing deal.

Choose a customer

Set this customer's carton, multipack and single-can retail prices, then see the margin on each beer at their current (or a "what-if") deal. Changing the deal here does not commit it — use Apply deal to save it as their standing deal.
Financial-year quarters (Q1 Jul–Sep … Q4 Apr–Jun). Volumes are shown in litres — kegs converted using keg size, cartons converted using each historical sale's actual can size — so keg and carton volume are directly comparable regardless of pack size. A "listing" means one beer actively being bought by one venue. Targets are set per RSM each quarter, shared live with your team; the Sales Team view automatically totals them. This all runs off your sales data, so it updates the moment you refresh it (e.g. your weekly Tuesday update).
Kegs use a 4-week cycle, cartons use a 13-week cycle — everything here is relative to now (your latest sales data), not a quarter you're browsing. New = ordered this cycle after 12+ months of nothing. Returned = missed last cycle, back this cycle. At risk = ordered last cycle, quiet this cycle — needs a call. Current = everyone active right now.
